title: Soy container candle -- cedarwood and vetiver
subtitle: A deep woody soy wax candle with cedarwood and vetiver essential oils
excerpt: Cedarwood and vetiver are base notes with long-lasting scent -- both survive the wax-pour temperature well enough to be used as essential oils rather than fragrance oil. The result is a warm, grounding wood candle without synthetic fragrance.
sourceNotes: Essential oil temperature stability in soy wax: standard candle formulation reference. Cedarwood and vetiver as base-note essential oils for candle blending: aromatherapy and candle-craft reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Add essential oils at 55°C to minimise flash-off.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for 48 hours before first burn. The cedarwood and vetiver deepen as the candle cures.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Candles keep for two years at room temperature. Discard if the surface turns oily or the scent is undetectable.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled from vetiver root. Deep, earthy, smoky-wood base note. Very viscous -- thick enough to need gentle warming before pouring. Provides exceptional longevity in candle blends.

content.text: Most essential oils evaporate too quickly at the temperatures needed to work with candle wax. Cedarwood and
are exceptions -- both are base notes that evaporate slowly and survive the pour temperature at the lower end of the working range. Cedarwood gives a warm wood note; vetiver adds earthy depth.

items.prepNote: about 3% of wax weight; 80 drops; warm the bottle slightly if it is very viscous
CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm diameter jar

content.text: Stick the wick tab to the centre of the jar base. Thread the wick through the centring bar or chopstick holder positioned across the jar rim.

Melt 140 g of soy wax in the pouring pitcher over a double boiler to 70 to 75°C. Remove from the heat and cool to 55 to 57°C -- lower than for fragrance oils to minimise essential oil evaporation. Add the cedarwood and vetiver essential oils by weight and stir slowly for 30 seconds. Pour into the jar immediately, at 55°C, filling to 1.5 cm below the rim.
Leave undisturbed for two hours. Trim the wick to 5 mm. Cure for 48 hours before lighting. The cold throw is light; the hot throw develops strongly after 30 minutes of burning.

Allow the first burn to reach a full melt pool across the jar. Trim the wick to 5 mm before every subsequent burn. Vetiver's low volatility means this candle develops stronger scent in the second half of its life, as the concentration of essential oil near the wick increases.
